dataset_profile.what_contracts: Open-source Solidity smart contract files with confirmed vulnerabilities. The final effectiveness benchmark contains 788 vulnerable contract files drawn from five source components after taxonomy mapping and validation.
dataset_profile.how_collected: The authors screened open-source Solidity datasets using availability, popularity/peer-review, and vulnerability-label criteria, retaining Not So Smart Contracts, SmartBugs-Curated, SolidiFI Benchmark, and Smart Contract Benchmark Suites. They also collected 2,941 representative BNB projects from BscScan and manually audited them with three security experts to construct the BNB Benchmark. Across the five components, files were deduplicated via MD5 after removing blank lines and comments, and the ground truth was manually relabeled and remapped to the new taxonomy at function level.
dataset_profile.scale: 788 vulnerable contract files in the final effectiveness benchmark; 10,394 ground-truth vulnerability instances; covers 40 of 45 taxonomy types.
dataset_profile.labeling: Manual function-level relabeling and taxonomy mapping with cross-validation by three security auditing experts. Source datasets originally varied in taxonomy and sometimes only labeled at file level; the authors normalized all retained benchmark data to function-level labels.
dataset_profile.label_count_basis: Total number of ground-truth vulnerability instances across the 788 vulnerable contract files in the final benchmark.

dataset_profile.what_contracts: Smart contract source codes with vulnerabilities confirmed through public security audit reports; available source codes were also compiled to support bytecode analysis.
dataset_profile.how_collected: Assembled from publicly available audit repositories maintained by Quantstamp, OpenZeppelin, Trail of Bits, ConsenSys, and CertiK, then mapped into vulnerability categories for benchmarking.
dataset_profile.labeling: Ground truth derived from external professional security audits. Source-code-based analysis was limited in practice; the paper's reported effectiveness results on AGT are primarily bytecode-based.
dataset_profile.label_granularity: Contract-level vulnerability-category labels derived from audit findings.
dataset_profile.label_count_basis: Number of audited smart contract source codes included in the AGT benchmark.

dataset_profile.what_contracts: Deduplicated smart contract source files derived from SmartBugs Wild, with deployed bytecode retrieved by address for bytecode-based evaluation.
dataset_profile.how_collected: Started from the SmartBugs Wild Dataset, removed duplicates and near-duplicates through AST-based normalization and hashing, then manually inspected contracts containing call/send/transfer patterns and annotated reentrancy conditions.
dataset_profile.scale: 47,398 initial SmartBugs Wild source codes; 22,237 after preprocessing/deduplication; 13,773 manually labeled contracts in the final benchmark.
dataset_profile.labeling: Manual expert annotation over more than four person-months by two master students supervised by a Ph.D. student. The benchmark labels reentrancy with subtype awareness for call, send, and transfer; contracts lacking these subtypes were considered non-vulnerable.
dataset_profile.label_granularity: Contract-level labels, with evaluation slices for call-only reentrancy and all-subtype reentrancy (call, send, transfer), on both source code and deployed bytecode representations.
dataset_profile.label_count_basis: Number of manually labeled smart contracts in the final RGT benchmark.

dataset_profile.what_contracts: Unique deduplicated Solidity smart contract source codes collected from Ethereum-related deployments.
dataset_profile.how_collected: Contract addresses were gathered via Google BigQuery. Source code was then downloaded from Etherscan or recovered from IPFS links embedded in contract bytecode metadata. Contracts were deduplicated by hashing smart contracts.
dataset_profile.labeling: Unlabeled; used for quantitative overlap/disagreement analysis across scanners rather than supervised ground-truth benchmarking.

dataset_profile.how_collected: Derived from local contract corpus and existing out/* tool reports.
dataset_profile.labeling: Ground truth derived from LAKresult CWE labels mapped to DASP categories via local mapping files.
dataset_profile.label_count_basis: contract-level DASP category labels; vulnerability_scores are TP/GT per category

dataset_profile.what_contracts: Contracts from smartbugsdb; child folders define vulnerability categories.
dataset_profile.how_collected: Derived from local SmartBugsDB corpus and existing smartbugsout/* tool reports.
dataset_profile.labeling: Ground truth derived from SmartBugsDB inline <yes><report> markers; short_addresses mapped to other.
dataset_profile.label_granularity: line-level labels aggregated to contract/category-level scoring
dataset_profile.label_count_basis: line-level SmartBugsDB labels aggregated by score_smartbugs_reports.py; vulnerability_scores are TP/GT per category
